ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_cases" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the test case */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of the test (e.g., unit test, integration test) */,
  "test_description" TEXT /* Description of the test case */,
  "test_suite_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test suite the test case belongs to */,
  "test_case_version_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the test case version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_test_suite_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_suite_id") REFERENCES test_suites ("suite_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_case_versions" (
  "test_case_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case version */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "test_case_version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the test case */,
  "test_case_version_description" TEXT /* Description of the test case version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test case version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test case version was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_case_version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_case_versions_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_case_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case parameter */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "parameter_value" TEXT /* Value of the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_case_parameters_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_suites" (
  "suite_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test suite */,
  "suite_name" TEXT /* Name of the test suite */,
  "suite_description" TEXT /* Description of the test suite */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the test suite is executed */,
  "suite_category_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the test suite category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("suite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_suites_environment_id FOREIGN KEY ("environment_id") REFERENCES environments ("environment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_suites_suite_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("suite_category_id") REFERENCES test_suite_categories ("suite_category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_suite_categories" (
  "suite_category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test suite category */,
  "suite_category_name" TEXT /* Name of the test suite category */,
  "suite_category_description" TEXT /* Description of the test suite category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("suite_category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environments" (
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environment */,
  "environment_name" TEXT /* Name of the environment */,
  "environment_description" TEXT /* Description of the environment */,
  "config_file_path" TEXT /* Path to the configuration file for the environment */,
  "environment_configuration_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the environment configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("environment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environments_environment_configuration_id FOREIGN KEY ("environment_configuration_id") REFERENCES environment_configurations ("environment_configuration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environment_configurations" (
  "environment_configuration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environment configuration */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment */,
  "configuration_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "configuration_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("environment_configuration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_steps" (
  "step_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test step */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case the step belongs to */,
  "step_number" INTEGER /* Number of the step in the test case */,
  "step_description" TEXT /* Description of the step */,
  "step_status" TEXT /* Status of the step (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "step_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the step in milliseconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("step_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_steps_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_executions" (
  "execution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test execution */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case being executed */,
  "test_suite_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test suite the test case belongs to */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the test is executed */,
  "execution_start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the test execution */,
  "execution_end_time" TEXT /* End time of the test execution */,
  "execution_status" TEXT /* Status of the test execution (e.g., in progress, completed, failed) */,
  "execution_log_file" TEXT /* Path to the log file for the test execution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("execution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_executions_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_executions_test_suite_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_suite_id") REFERENCES test_suites ("suite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_executions_environment_id FOREIGN KEY ("environment_id") REFERENCES environments ("environment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_execution_history" (
  "execution_history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test execution history */,
  "execution_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test execution */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "test_suite_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test suite the test case belongs to */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the test is executed */,
  "execution_start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the test execution */,
  "execution_end_time" TEXT /* End time of the test execution */,
  "execution_status" TEXT /* Status of the test execution (e.g., in progress, completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("execution_history_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, QA engineer, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions for the user (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the test report or test case */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case being accessed */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test report being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the test report or test case was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_report_attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test report */,
  "attachment_name" TEXT /* Name of the attachment */,
  "attachment_file" TEXT /* File contents of the attachment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "execution_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test execution */,
  "total_steps" INTEGER /* Total number of steps in the test execution */,
  "total_pass" INTEGER /* Total number of steps that passed */,
  "total_fail" INTEGER /* Total number of steps that failed */,
  "total_fatal" INTEGER /* Total number of steps that resulted in a fatal error */,
  "total_error" INTEGER /* Total number of steps that resulted in an error */,
  "total_warning" INTEGER /* Total number of steps with warnings */,
  "total_info" INTEGER /* Total number of steps with informational messages */,
  "total_skip" INTEGER /* Total number of steps skipped */,
  "total_unknown" INTEGER /* Total number of steps with unknown status */,
  "execution_time" INTEGER /* Total execution time in milliseconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_metrics_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_metrics_execution_id FOREIGN KEY ("execution_id") REFERENCES test_executions ("execution_id")
)
